free_text: Measurement of fusion-evaporation cross sections for
reactions of 44Ca with 154,156,157,160Gd targets
Expriment performed at the K500
superconducting cyclotron using Momentum Achromat
Recoil Spectrometer (MARS) .
Evaporation residue were first momentum
analyzed in MARS spectrometer, consisting of two
dipole magnets, Wien filter and several correcting
quadrupoles and sextupoles.
At the MARS focal plane a position sensitive
Si strip detector was placed. Evaporation residues
were implanted in SiSD and identified acording their
alpha decay energies.
Evaporation residues were implanted in Si
strip detector and identified by their characteristic
alpha decay energies.
Systematic uncertainty, determined mainly by
low MARS transmision efficiency at low energies
Statistical uncertainty
Decay data measured by author.
Compiled by S.H.
Table 3
The targets were prepared in
house using the molecular plating method. The Gd(NO3)3
solution was plated onto a 2 mu-m Ti backing by
applying a bias of 400-700 V for 30-60 min. The targets
were then baked at 200 deg C under atmospheric
conditions.
No answer from C.M.Foldern III about the isomeric
states considered.
